expectations.text: Recognizes that no profile exists and setup is needed
expectations.evidence: Step 1 runs test-admin-profile.sh, receives {"exists":false,...}, and explicitly states: 'Profile does not exist. Must run TUI setup interview before proceeding.'

expectations.text: Asks the TUI interview questions (storage location, preferences, inventory scan)
expectations.evidence: Step 2 covers all three interview sections: Q1 asks about storage location (single vs multi device), Q2 asks about tool preferences (package manager, Python manager, Node manager, default shell), and Q3 asks about running an inventory scan.

expectations.text: Passes uv as Python manager and pnpm as Node manager to setup
expectations.evidence: The new-admin-profile.sh command in Step 3 includes '--py-mgr "uv"' and '--node-mgr "pnpm"', matching the user's stated preferences from the interview.

expectations.text: Uses new-admin-profile.sh with correct flags (--py-mgr, --node-mgr, etc.)
expectations.evidence: Step 3 shows the full command: 'scripts/new-admin-profile.sh --admin-root "$HOME/.admin" --pkg-mgr "apt" --py-mgr "uv" --node-mgr "pnpm" --shell-default "bash" --run-inventory' with all expected flags.

expectations.text: Re-runs test-admin-profile.sh to verify profile was created
expectations.evidence: Step 4 explicitly re-runs 'scripts/test-admin-profile.sh' and expects output with '"exists":true' to confirm profile creation.

notes: The transcript is a structured walkthrough rather than a live session capture. All steps are described in the expected order with correct commands, flags, and verification. The agent follows the CLAUDE.md profile gate protocol, conducts the full TUI interview, creates the profile with user-specified preferences, and verifies the result.
